description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane seaplane with a striking color scheme of orange-red and blue-gray. The aircraft features two large, straight wings stacked vertically with visible struts and rigging wires, and two prominent piston engines mounted between the wings, each with a propeller. The fuselage is streamlined and features a painted shark mouth motif at the nose, adding a distinctive and aggressive appearance. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, and the rear fuselage tapers to a point. The airplane is equipped with large floats beneath the fuselage, indicating its capability for water landings and takeoffs. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with windows visible.
description: A vintage biplane seaplane with orange-red and blue-gray coloring, twin piston engines, and large floats for water landings. It features a shark mouth nose and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stacked biplane wings, twin engines, and the streamlined fuselage with the shark mouth nose art.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin propeller engines, wide wingspan, and the aggressive shark mouth design on the nose.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same biplane structure, engine placement, and tail configuration as view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail assembly, twin floats, and the alignment of the wings and engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings connected by struts and rigging; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted between the upper and lower wings, each with a tractor propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Float landing gear with two large main floats for water operations; no visible wheels.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely accommodating one or two seats.
